campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also heard public comments on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on government code section 3502
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comment period on council reports

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved moving forward with an engineer's report for the fiscal year and scheduled an ordinance for final adoption on May 20th. The council also discussed whether the city should explore becoming a charter city and reviewed preliminary estimates for potential new tax options and revenue sources.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report approval for fiscal year operations
Public hearing and ordinance adoption scheduled for May 20, 2026
Exploring charter city status for the city
Preliminary tax and revenue options under review

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, confirm a youth commissioner appointment, and set up the November 2026 municipal election for three open seats. The council also approved new development maps, renewed software agreements, and authorized budget changes.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general municipal election for three town council seats
New youth commissioner appointment
Final approval of Tract 10675 development map (Boulevard location)
Five-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. for town services
Budget and expenditure authorizations

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 3, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize upcoming agenda items for two future meetings. Council members reviewed which new items to add, scheduled specific times for discussions, and approved free use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for an event on June 7.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda for June 9, 2026 council meeting
Organized draft agenda for June 16, 2026 meeting
Approved free use of Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for June 7 event

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 4, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 4, 2026 with both in-person and virtual participation options via Zoom. Residents could join online or by phone to watch the meeting and submit public comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available through Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment period for residents to address the City Council
Instructions for raising hand and unmuting to speak during the meeting

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 2, 2026. The first was a closed-door session (details not publicly disclosed), followed by a second special meeting with items designated as Special Order of the Day.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held at 5:30 PM
Special Order of the Day items discussed at 6:30 PM
Two consecutive special meetings scheduled on same evening

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 2, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to approve proclamations celebrating LGBTQ+ Pride Month in June and recognizing the United Nations World Environment Day on June 5th. The meeting also included an open public comment period where residents could address the council on any city matters not on the official agenda.
keyTopics: Proclamation declaring June as LGBTQ+ Pride Month
Proclamation recognizing UN World Environment Day (June 5)
Public comment period for residents on non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 3, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding Charter Review Committee meetings to discuss potential changes to the city's governing document. The city is offering both in-person and remote participation options so residents can attend and provide feedback.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings scheduled (in-person)
Remote participation available via Zoom for those unable to attend in person
Public can submit written comments by email up to 10 a.m. the day before meetings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 2, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's budget plan for fiscal year 2026-27 and handle routine administrative matters like approving previous meeting minutes and scheduling future meetings. The council also discussed changes to how city commissions will handle appointment cycles going forward.
keyTopics: Approval of the 2026-27 city operating budget
Commission appointment process changes to quarterly cycles
City Council meeting calendar and schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 27, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to review the city's 5-year capital improvement and spending plan for 2027-2031, discuss whether the city qualifies for a state prohousing designation program, and get an update on downtown parking modernization efforts. The council considered recommendations to move forward on the capital plan and studied materials for the prohousing program application.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan approval and financing
Prohousing Designation Program application review
Downtown parking modernization initiatives
Comprehensive Plan compliance for capital projects
